pairs.question: What was the purpose of the Conservative Philosophy Group (CPG) when it was founded in 1974?
pairs.answer: The Conservative Philosophy Group (CPG) was established in 1974 to provide an intellectual foundation for conservatism in the UK. It aimed to counterbalance the ideological framework of opposing political groups, particularly during a period when the Conservative Party had recently lost two general elections and was seeking a new direction under leader Margaret Thatcher.

pairs.question: Who were the founding members of the Conservative Philosophy Group (CPG) in 1974?
pairs.answer: The founding members of the CPG were Sir Hugh Fraser, Roger Scruton, John Casey, and Jonathan Aitken MP.

pairs.question: Which notable figures outside the CPG's board addressed the group during its initial period?
pairs.answer: Several prominent figures addressed the CPG, including economists F. A. Hayek and Milton Friedman, philosopher Michael Oakeshott, historian Elie Kedourie, and former Prime Minister Harold Macmillan.

pairs.question: What role did Margaret Thatcher play in the Conservative Philosophy Group (CPG)?
pairs.answer: Margaret Thatcher attended meetings of the CPG on multiple occasions. According to Jonathan Aitken, she emphasized the need for the Conservative Party to develop its own ideology to guide policy-making, stating, 'We must have an ideology; the other side have got an ideology they can test their policies against. We must have one as well.'

pairs.question: When and why did the Conservative Philosophy Group (CPG) disband?
pairs.answer: The CPG disbanded during the tenure of John Major as Prime Minister. The exact reasons for its disbandment are not specified, but it likely reflected shifting political priorities or the resolution of the intellectual needs the group was created to address.

pairs.question: How was the Conservative Philosophy Group (CPG) restarted in 2013, and who were the key figures involved?
pairs.answer: The CPG was restarted in 2013 by Professor Roger Scruton, with assistance from Rodney Leach and other academics. It was relaunched in partnership with the organisers of the Conservative Renewal Conferences, George Bathurst and Richard Hyslop. The group continued to meet in locations such as the home of Lord Flight in Westminster.

pairs.question: Write a Python snippet to generate a list of all known CPG members, including those who were board members, regular attendees, or guest speakers.
pairs.answer:
python
cpg_members = {
    "Founding Members": [
        "Sir Hugh Fraser",
        "Roger Scruton",
        "John Casey",
        "Jonathan Aitken MP"
    ],
    "Other Members": [
        "Alan Clark MP",
        "Enoch Powell MP",
        "Maurice Cowling",
        "Edward Norman",
        "Sir Alfred Sherman",
        "Paul Johnson",
        "T. E. Utley",
        "Lord Black",
        "Lord Bauer",
        "Lord Blake",
        "Lord Sieff",
        "Lord Weinstock",
        "Lord Thomas",
        "Lord Quinton"
    ],
    "Guest Speakers": [
        "F. A. Hayek",
        "Milton Friedman",
        "Michael Oakeshott",
        "Elie Kedourie",
        "Harold Macmillan"
    ]
}

# Example usage:
for category, members in cpg_members.items():
    print(f"{category}: {', '.join(members)}")
